title: Freezing the Notebook: An Interventional Invariant of Capability Formation Across Substrates
description:
An interventional invariant of capability formation, tested across toy physics, purpose-built mini-networks, and pretrained transformers (GPT-2, Pythia, Qwen2.5, OLMo-2).

Define an invariant as a triplet (gesture, signature, kill criterion): an intervention performable identically across substrates, a predicted effect, and a pre-registered outcome that would falsify the claim. The candidate tested here: freezing σ, the slow variable a system writes and rereads (its "notebook"), collapses the capability that σ carries — at inference for an installed capability, and, applied during training, it prevents the capability from ever forming. Across seven capability types and one crossing of the implicit↔explicit divide, the invariant was never falsified. Several of the authors' own hypotheses were, and are reported alongside a training-time causality artifact that was discovered, fixed, and is now guarded against automatically.

Two doors: a non-technical walk in ten chapters, and a formal paper. Every claim maps to a runnable command with a pinned environment and registered seeds; the core collapse runs on a laptop in minutes.

Code is MIT; texts and figures are CC BY 4.0.
notes: Published under a pseudonym by choice. Please do not add ORCID, affiliation, or contributor metadata to this record without reading meta/discovery-preregistration.md in the repository first: this file is deliberately free of identifying fields.
